how_it_works.title: How Paprika Works
how_it_works.description: Paprika is a unified research tool that connects you to the world’s academic papers by bridging multiple open-source databases.
how_it_works.content_1: Instead of relying on a single library, Paprika runs massive parallel queries against the world's largest open-access graphs, such as OpenAlex and Crossref, combining their results seamlessly.
how_it_works.content_2: Every academic API has completely different structures. We clean up and normalize these differences behind the scenes, presenting each paper in a clear, universal design—complete with citations, abstract, authors, and open-access status.
how_it_works.content_3: We don’t just use keyword matching. By leveraging advanced semantic relevance scores (via algorithms like OpenAlex), Paprika strives to present the most significant and relevant findings first.
